guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Antenatal folate supplementation (folic acid 500 microgram daily preconception and first trimester)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance: a dramatic panic-attack / tachycardia / tachypnoea 'physiological crisis' narrative frames a physiologically stable, benign early-pregnancy encounter. The scored entity is the benign one; escalating the resolved panic to ED/000 is the primary error of commission.

Bidirectional label-red-herring: the benign administrative title (serum beta-hCG testing + counselling) is genuinely benign in the note (Risk Level Low, stable vitals-congruent-with-panic, GP review in 48h), NOT a benign title masking an acute entity — resist the reflex to uplift.

Important-not-to-miss safety net: ectopic pregnancy is screened-negative now (no unilateral pain, no bleeding, no shoulder-tip pain, stable BP) but pregnancy location is unconfirmed pending dating ultrasound — the AI must hold the 000 ectopic-rupture safety net without over-triaging tonight.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who palpated the abdomen, performed a point-of-care urine beta-hCG, and ordered serum bloods + dating ultrasound. Breath-Ezy cannot examine, cannot run the POC/serum test, and cannot image — the AI-correct action is to arrange the prompt in-person GP/pathology/ultrasound pathway, provide panic support, and safety-net for ectopic.
Age not stated in source — inferred adult (~29) reproductive-age female; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source note GPO-040.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
